PatientDocument(file_path='data/patient_demo/patient_notes.pdf', page_number=3, note_type='discharge_summary', raw_text='DISCHARGE SUMMARY
DIAGNOSIS:
1) ACUTE GASTROENTERITIS WITH DEHYDRATION
2) URINARY TRACT INFECTION
HISTORY: C/O Multiple episodes of loose stools, 2-3 episodes of vomiting,
fatigue since 3 days and fever since yesterday.
Initially she was treated at local clinic.
PAST HISTORY: K/C/O Thyroid disorder on treatment.
PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
PR-89/min, BP-130/80 mmHg, RR-20/min, SPO2-98% at room air.
CNS-Conscious Oriented
CVS-S1S2(+)
RS-B/L NVBS(+)
PA-Soft, non tender
COURSE IN THE HOSPITAL:
Patient presented to us with above mentioned complaints. After evaluation, she
was admitted to ward. Initial investigations showed normal CBC. Serum
creatinine (1.65mg/dl) elevated. Serum electrolytes showed low
serum sodium (128.00mmol/L). Urine routine showed ketone bodies(+), 10-
12/hpf of pus cells, 15-20/hpf of epithelial cells with presence of bacteria hence
urine culture and sensitivity sent - report awaited.
She was treated with IV fluids, IV antibiotics, IV PPIs, IV antiemetics.
USG abdomen showed Grade-I fatty liver changes.
Repeat Serum Creatinine (1.17mg/dl) done was normal.
Patient was advised to stay back but attendants not willing, hence being discharged at request.
CONDITION AT DISCHARGE: Hemodynamically stable
ADVICE ON DISCHARGE:
1. TAB. RACIPER 40MG - 1-0-0 - 7 DAYS (BEFORE FOOD)
2. TAB. EMESET 4MG - 1-1-1 - 3 DAYS
3. TAB. OFLOX TZ - 1-0-1 - 5 DAYS
4. TAB M STRONG - 1-0-0 - 15 DAYS
5. TAB. ZEDOTT - 1-1-1 - 3 DAYS
6. TAB. LOPIRAMIDE 2MG - 1-0-1 - 5 DAYS
FOLLOW-UP INSTRUCTIONS:
Urine culture and sensitivity sent - report awaited.
Review immediately in case of fever, loose stools, vomiting, fatigue.
Review on 09.03.2026. CBC
Date of Discharge: 09/03/2026', extraction_confidence=1.0, read_error=None)

data.raw_text:
CASE RECORD / ADMISSION RECORD (1)
Date of Admission: 26/02/2026
Chief Complaints: Fever, Generalized weakness Since 3 days, Myalgia.
History of Present Illness: Patient on his regular medication.
Gradually c/o Fever, Myalgia, Generalized weakness for further Rx came to our hospital.
Past History / Drug History: K/c/o T2DM (on Ayurvedic Medication)
HbA1c - 13.9% (outside report)
Allergic History: Not Known
CASE RECORD / ADMISSION RECORD (2)
Examination: O/E Conscious, oriented
PR: 116 b/m  BP: 87/50 mmHg  RR: 26 b/m  SPO2: 99%
GRBS: 443 mg/dL  Temp: 98.3°F
Diagnosis (Provisional): T2DM AKI Uncontrolled T2DM
Final Diagnosis:
- ? Synovitis
- Cholelithiasis without Cholecystitis

HAEMATOLOGY REPORT
COMPLETE BLOOD COUNT (CBC)
HAEMOGLOBIN (HB): 11.4 gm/dl   Reference: 13.5-17.5
TOTAL COUNT: 7160 Cells/cumm    Reference: 4000-11000
NEUTROPHILS: 79.1 %             Reference: 35-66
LYMPHOCYTES: 12.1 %
PLATELET COUNT: 1.80 Lakhs/cumm
PACKED CELL VOLUME (PCV): 35.9 %
BIOCHEMISTRY REPORT
SERUM CREATININE: 0.85 mg/dL    Reference: 0.7-1.4
RANDOM BLOOD SUGAR (RBS): 496.00 mg/dL  Reference: 80-140
SERUM ELECTROLYTES
S.SODIUM: 127.00 mmol/L         Reference: 135-150
S.POTASSIUM (K+): 3.84 mmol/L   Reference: 3.5-5
ABG WITH LACTATE
pH: 7.39
pCO2: 32.00 mmHg
HCO3: 19.20 mmol/L              Reference: 20-28
Urine culture and sensitivity sent - report awaited.

DISCHARGE SUMMARY
DIAGNOSIS:
1) ACUTE GASTROENTERITIS WITH DEHYDRATION
2) URINARY TRACT INFECTION
HISTORY: C/O Multiple episodes of loose stools, 2-3 episodes of vomiting,
fatigue since 3 days and fever since yesterday.
Initially she was treated at local clinic.
PAST HISTORY: K/C/O Thyroid disorder on treatment.
PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
PR-89/min, BP-130/80 mmHg, RR-20/min, SPO2-98% at room air.
CNS-Conscious Oriented
CVS-S1S2(+)
RS-B/L NVBS(+)
PA-Soft, non tender
COURSE IN THE HOSPITAL:
Patient presented to us with above mentioned complaints. After evaluation, she
was admitted to ward. Initial investigations showed normal CBC. Serum
creatinine (1.65mg/dl) elevated. Serum electrolytes showed low
serum sodium (128.00mmol/L). Urine routine showed ketone bodies(+), 10-
12/hpf of pus cells, 15-20/hpf of epithelial cells with presence of bacteria hence
urine culture and sensitivity sent - report awaited.
She was treated with IV fluids, IV antibiotics, IV PPIs, IV antiemetics.
USG abdomen showed Grade-I fatty liver changes.
Repeat Serum Creatinine (1.17mg/dl) done was normal.
Patient was advised to stay back but attendants not willing, hence being discharged at request.
CONDITION AT DISCHARGE: Hemodynamically stable
ADVICE ON DISCHARGE:
1. TAB. RACIPER 40MG - 1-0-0 - 7 DAYS (BEFORE FOOD)
2. TAB. EMESET 4MG - 1-1-1 - 3 DAYS
3. TAB. OFLOX TZ - 1-0-1 - 5 DAYS
4. TAB M STRONG - 1-0-0 - 15 DAYS
5. TAB. ZEDOTT - 1-1-1 - 3 DAYS
6. TAB. LOPIRAMIDE 2MG - 1-0-1 - 5 DAYS
FOLLOW-UP INSTRUCTIONS:
Urine culture and sensitivity sent - report awaited.
Review immediately in case of fever, loose stools, vomiting, fatigue.
Review on 09.03.2026. CBC
Date of Discharge: 09/03/2026

DRUG CHART (1)
Diagnosis: T2DM AFI
Allergies: Not Known
Drug: INJ MEROPENEM
Dose: 1gm  Route: IV  Freq: 1-1-1  Date: 28/2/26
Drug: INJ PAN (Pantoprazole)
Dose: 40mg  Route: IV  Freq: OD  Date: 28/2/26
Drug: INJ EMESET
Dose: 4mg  Route: IV  Freq: 1-1-1  Date: 28/2/26
Drug: INJ LANTUS (Insulin Glargine)
Dose: 10U  Route: S/C  Freq: OD  Date: 28/2/26
TAB DOLO (Paracetamol)
Dose: 650mg  Route: P/O  Freq: 1-1-1  Date: 28/2/26
